intro: Leptochilus fendleri, also known as Fendler's polypody or Fendler's maidenhair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Leptochilus fendleri include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Leptochilus fendleri is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and salves for skin conditions and wounds.
